senses.gloss: Caesalpinieae tribe plants used for their brazilin-containing woods

body.text: wood of the Braziltree, a species of Cæsalpinia;
wood from which a well-known dye is prepared;

wood of a certain great tree, the leaves of which are like those of the almond, and having a red stem, the decoction of which is used as a dye: it consolidates wounds, stops a flow of blood from any member, and dries up ulcers; and its root,
lowest part, is an instantaneous poison:
(K:) the word is said by some to be Arabic; (Msb;) others say that it is arabicized; (S, Msb, TA;) [perhaps from the Persian

;] and that the only other words of the same measure in the Arabic language are proper names, and four in number, (S TA,) or seven: (TA:) if used as a proper name, it is imperfectly decl., because determinate and of the measure of a verb. (S.)
